title: Blood Relations
subtitle: Family Trees, Coded Relations, Pointing/Indicating, Puzzle-based Inferences

Wipro.style: Direct pointing or basic family tree

readingSections.title: What are Blood Relations? 🧬
readingSections.quickSummary: Blood relations are all about figuring out how people are connected through family! Think of it like building a family tree from the roots to the leaves.
readingSections.whyThisMatters: Every major IT company asks 2-4 blood relation questions. They test your logical deduction and ability to track multiple relationships without getting confused.
readingSections.pattern: You'll see a scenario where Person A points to Person B, or a coded sentence like 'A + B means A is the father of B'. Your job is to find the exact relationship.
readingSections.content: Blood relation questions test your ability to map out family connections[reference:0]. You must track generations (grandparents, parents, children), genders (male/female), and specific roles (brother, sister, uncle, aunt). These relations can be direct like parents and children, or indirect like uncles, aunts, and cousins[reference:1]. The golden rule is to never assume genders unless explicitly stated, and always draw a family tree[reference:2]!

subsections.title: The Golden Rule: Draw it Out!
subsections.content: Never try to solve these purely in your head. The human brain gets tangled in 'my mother's brother's son'. Always draw a quick family tree on your rough sheet[reference:3].
subsections.example: If the question says 'A is the brother of B', draw A and B on the same horizontal line. If A is male, put a '+' or square. If B's gender is unknown, put a '?'[reference:4].

subsections.title: Generations Matter
subsections.content: Always figure out which generation each person belongs to[reference:5]. Grandparents are +2, Parents/Uncles/Aunts are +1, Self/Siblings/Cousins are 0, Children/Nieces/Nephews are -1, Grandchildren are -2[reference:6].
subsections.example: If A is the father of B, A is generation +1, B is generation 0. You can never jump generations unless stated.

subsections.title: The Gender Trap
subsections.content: Never assume the gender of a person based on their name or profession unless the question explicitly states it[reference:7]. 'Doctor' can be male or female.
subsections.example: If the question says 'A is the doctor and B is his wife', A is male. But if it just says 'A is a doctor', A's gender is unknown until revealed[reference:8].

subsections.title: Blood Relation vs Marriage Relations
subsections.content: Blood relations are connections by birth. Marriage relations (in-laws) are connections created through marriage[reference:9]. Both are tested in exams!
subsections.example: Your mother is a blood relation. Your mother-in-law is a marriage relation. Both can appear in questions[reference:10].

quickRevision: Always draw a family tree!
Track generations (+2, +1, 0, -1, -2)
Never assume gender unless stated
Break complex sentences into small parts
Use symbols: + for male, - for female, = for marriage, | for parent-child
readingSections.companyNote: TCS and Wipro love simple pointing questions. Infosys and Accenture love coded relations. Practice both[reference:11]!

readingSections.title: Types of Blood Relation Questions 🔍
readingSections.quickSummary: Questions usually come in three flavors: Pointing (who is in the photo?), Coded (math-like symbols), and Puzzles (a whole family story).
readingSections.whyThisMatters: Knowing the type tells you exactly how to solve it. Pointing needs backward tracking. Coded needs symbol decoding. Puzzles need a full family tree[reference:12].
readingSections.pattern: Pointing: 'Pointing to a man, a woman said...' | Coded: 'A $ B means A is the mother of B' | Puzzle: 'There is a family of 6 people...'
readingSections.content: Understanding the format saves time[reference:13]. Pointing questions are solved by working backward from the end of the sentence. Coded questions are solved by replacing symbols with actual relations[reference:14]. Puzzle questions require drawing a complete chart[reference:15].

subsections.title: Type 1: Pointing / Indicating
subsections.content: Someone points to a photo or a person and describes them[reference:16]. Solve by starting from the last relation mentioned and working backward to the speaker[reference:17].
subsections.example: 'Pointing to a photo, Raj says: She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' Start from the end: 'grandfather's only son' = Raj's father. So, 'She is the daughter of my father' = Raj's sister.[reference:18]

subsections.title: Type 2: Coded Relations
subsections.content: Relations are hidden behind symbols (+, -, *, /) or words[reference:19]. You must decode the symbol first, then chain them together[reference:20].
subsections.example: 'A + B' means A is the father of B. 'A - B' means A is the sister of B. If we have P + Q - R, it means P is the father of Q, and Q is the sister of R. Therefore, P is the father of R.[reference:21]

subsections.title: Type 3: Family Puzzles
subsections.content: A paragraph describes a family with conditions (e.g., 'There are two married couples', 'No two males sit together')[reference:22]. You must build the whole tree[reference:23].
subsections.example: Use clues one by one. First, place the definite couples. Then add children. Use elimination for unknown genders.[reference:24]

quickRevision: Pointing = Start from the end of the sentence
Coded = Decode symbols first, then chain them
Puzzle = Draw the full tree step-by-step using clues
Always check for 'only son', 'only daughter' traps
Verify your final answer by reading it forward
readingSections.companyNote: Infosys heavily tests Coded Relations. TCS NQT prefers Pointing. Wipro uses simple Puzzles[reference:25].

readingSections.title: Generational Mapping & Symbols 🗺️
readingSections.quickSummary: A family tree has levels (generations) and lines (relationships). Master the symbols to map anyone instantly!
readingSections.whyThisMatters: If you don't know the standard symbols, you'll waste time drawing messy trees. Standard symbols make it visual and fast[reference:26].
readingSections.pattern: Horizontal line (-) = Siblings. Vertical line (|) = Parent to Child. Double horizontal line (=) = Married Couple. Box/Square = Male. Circle = Female.
readingSections.content: Think of a family tree like a building[reference:27]. The top floor is Grandparents (+2 generation). The middle floor is Parents, Uncles, Aunts (+1 generation). The ground floor is You, Siblings, Cousins (0 generation). The basement is Children, Nieces, Nephews (-1 generation)[reference:28].

subsections.title: The Symbols Cheat Sheet
subsections.content: Male: Square [ ] or (+). Female: Circle ( ) or (-). Marriage: Double line (=). Siblings: Single horizontal line (-). Parent-Child: Single vertical line (|)[reference:29][reference:30].
subsections.example: To draw 'A is the father of B, and B is the sister of C': Draw A(+) at the top. Draw a vertical line down to B(-) and C(?). Draw a horizontal line between B and C.[reference:31]

subsections.title: Maternal vs Paternal
subsections.content: Paternal = Father's side (Paternal Uncle = Father's brother). Maternal = Mother's side (Maternal Uncle = Mother's brother)[reference:32].
subsections.example: If the question says 'My mother's father', it's your Maternal Grandfather. 'My father's father' is your Paternal Grandfather.[reference:33]

subsections.title: In-Law Relations
subsections.content: Spouse's parents = Father-in-law / Mother-in-law. Spouse's siblings = Brother-in-law / Sister-in-law. Sibling's spouse = Also Brother/Sister-in-law.[reference:34][reference:35]
subsections.example: Your wife's brother is your Brother-in-law. Your sister's husband is also your Brother-in-law. They are on the same generation level as you.[reference:36]

quickRevision: Paternal = Father's side | Maternal = Mother's side
In-laws are always the same generation as you
Niece = Brother/Sister's daughter | Nephew = Brother/Sister's son
Cousin = Uncle/Aunt's child (same generation as you)
Grand-nephew = Nephew's son
readingSections.companyNote: Accenture loves testing Maternal vs Paternal distinctions to trick students who just guess 'Uncle'[reference:37].

readingSections.title: 5 Question Patterns You Must Know 🎯
readingSections.quickSummary: Every blood relation question fits a pattern. Recognize it, apply the trick, and solve in 30 seconds!
readingSections.whyThisMatters: Patterns give you a ready-made strategy. No more staring at the screen wondering where to start[reference:38].
readingSections.pattern: Look at the question structure. Is it a pointing question? Coded? A puzzle? Each has a specific 3-step method[reference:39].
readingSections.content: We've categorized all campus test blood relation questions into 5 core patterns[reference:40]. Master these, and you can solve any variation.

subsections.title: Pattern 1: The 'Backward Tracking' Pointing
subsections.content: Question: 'Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the son of my father's only daughter.' How is the man related to the woman?[reference:41]
subsections.example: Step 1: Start from the end. 'My father's only daughter'. Since the speaker is a woman, her father's only daughter is HERSELF. Step 2: Replace it. 'He is the son of myself'. Step 3: Answer = Her son. ✅[reference:42]

subsections.title: Pattern 2: The 'Coded Chain'
subsections.content: Question: If A $ B means A is brother of B, A @ B means A is wife of B. Find relation in P $ Q @ R.[reference:43]
subsections.example: Step 1: P $ Q -> P is brother of Q. Step 2: Q @ R -> Q is wife of R. Step 3: Chain it -> P is the brother of Q, and Q is the wife of R. Therefore, P is the brother-in-law of R. ✅[reference:44]

subsections.title: Pattern 3: The 'Only Son/Daughter' Trap
subsections.content: Question: 'I have no brother or sister, but that man's son is my father's son.'[reference:45]
subsections.example: Step 1: 'I have no brother or sister' -> I am an only child. Step 2: 'My father's son' -> Since I am the only child, 'my father's son' is ME. Step 3: 'That man's son is ME' -> I am the son of that man. So the man in the photo is my FATHER. ✅[reference:46]

subsections.title: Pattern 4: The 'Multi-Generation Puzzle'
subsections.content: Question: A family of 6. A and B are a couple. C is their son. D is C's wife. E is D's brother. How is E related to A?[reference:47]
subsections.example: Step 1: Draw A=B (Couple). Step 2: C is their son (A=B -> C). Step 3: D is C's wife (C=D). Step 4: E is D's brother (E-D). Step 5: E is the brother of C's wife.[reference:48]

subsections.title: Pattern 5: The 'Gender Unknown' Trick
subsections.content: Question: A is the child of B. But B is not the father of A. Who is B?[reference:49]
subsections.example: Step 1: A is the child of B. So B is a parent. Step 2: B is NOT the father. Step 3: If B is a parent but not the father, B MUST be the MOTHER. ✅ (Classic trick!)[reference:50]

quickRevision: Pointing = Start from the last word, work backward
Coded = Decode symbol by symbol, draw mini-trees
Only son/daughter = The speaker is usually the person!
Puzzles = Draw the tree step-by-step, don't skip clues
Not father + parent = Mother. Not mother + parent = Father.
readingSections.companyNote: TCS NQT loves Pattern 1 & 3. Infosys loves Pattern 2. Wipro uses Pattern 4[reference:51].

readingSections.title: Quick Reference: The Blood Relation Method ✅
readingSections.quickSummary: Your ultimate cheat sheet to solve any blood relation question in under 45 seconds.
readingSections.whyThisMatters: Speed and accuracy are everything. This method eliminates guesswork and prevents silly mistakes[reference:52].
readingSections.content: Follow these 4 steps every single time[reference:53]. They work for all patterns and guarantee accuracy.

subsections.title: Step 1: Read & Identify
subsections.content: Read the question. Is it Pointing, Coded, or a Puzzle? This decides your approach[reference:54].
subsections.example: If you see 'Pointing to...', use Backward Tracking. If you see symbols like '+, -, *', use Coded Chain.[reference:55]

subsections.title: Step 2: Break it Down
subsections.content: For pointing, break the sentence at 'my', 'his', 'her'. For coded, write the meaning of each symbol next to it[reference:56].
subsections.example: 'My mother's husband's only son' -> 'My mother's husband' = My father. 'My father's only son' = Me (if I'm male) or My brother.[reference:57]

subsections.title: Step 3: Draw the Mini-Tree
subsections.content: Use your rough sheet[reference:58]. Draw boxes for males, circles for females. Connect them[reference:59]. Don't rely on memory[reference:60].
subsections.example: A(+) = B(-) -> C(+). This instantly shows A and B are married, C is their son.[reference:61]

subsections.title: Step 4: The 'Reverse Check'
subsections.content: Once you find the answer, read the relationship backward to see if it makes sense[reference:62].
subsections.example: If you conclude 'He is my Uncle', check: 'Is my mother's brother my Uncle?' Yes. If it fits, you're 100% correct.[reference:63]

quickRevision: Identify type first (Pointing/Coded/Puzzle)
Break sentences at 'my/his/her'
ALWAYS draw a mini-tree on rough sheet
Use + for male, - for female, = for marriage
Reverse check your final answer
readingSections.companyNote: Students who draw trees score 100% in Blood Relations. Students who do it in their head score 50%. Draw the tree![reference:64]

formulas.title: Family Tree Symbols & Generations
formulas.formula: Male = □ or (+) | Female = ○ or (-) | Marriage = = | Sibling = — | Parent-Child = |
formulas.whenToUse: For ALL blood relation questions. This is your foundational tool.
formulas.theTrick: Draw males as squares/plus, females as circles/minus. Keep generations in horizontal rows. Top row = Grandparents (+2), Middle = Parents (+1), Bottom = Children (0) and grandchildren (-1).
formulas.explanation: Think of it like a corporate org chart. The CEO (Grandpa) is at the top. Managers (Parents) are in the middle. Employees (You) are at the bottom. Lines show who reports to whom (parent to child) or who is on the same level (siblings). Always maintain generation levels.[reference:7][reference:8]
formulas.example: A is the father of B and C. B is married to D. Draw A(+) at the top. Draw a line down to B(+) and C(?). Draw B(=)D(-).
formulas.commonMistake: Putting spouses on different vertical levels. Spouses (husband and wife) MUST be on the exact same horizontal level, connected by a double line.[reference:9]

practiceProblems.question: If A is the father of B, and B is the sister of C, what is the relation of A to C?
practiceProblems.explanation: Draw A(+) at top, B(-) and C(?) on same row with a horizontal line between them (siblings). A is connected vertically to B and C, so A is the parent of both. Since A is male and father of B, he is also father of C.[reference:10]

practiceProblems.question: If X is the mother of Y, and Y is the brother of Z, what is X to Z?
practiceProblems.explanation: Draw X(-) above Y(+) and Z(?) connected as siblings; X is parent of both, so X is mother of Z.

practiceProblems.question: If P is husband of Q, and Q is sister of R, how is P related to R?
practiceProblems.explanation: Draw P(+) and Q(-) as spouses on same level; Q and R are siblings on same level. P is brother-in-law of R (sister's husband).[reference:11]

practiceProblems.question: If A is son of B, B is daughter of C, then A is C's?
practiceProblems.explanation: C is grandparent of A (since B is child of C and A is child of B). A is male, so grandson.[reference:12]

practiceProblems.question: If M is father of N, N is mother of O, then M is O's?
practiceProblems.explanation: M is father of N, N is mother of O; M is grandparent of O, male so grandfather.[reference:13]

formulas.title: Pointing/Indicating Backward Rule
formulas.formula: Start from the LAST relation mentioned → Replace with the speaker → Move backward to the first relation.
formulas.whenToUse: When the question starts with 'Pointing to a photograph/person, X said...'[reference:14]
formulas.theTrick: Never start from the beginning of the sentence! Start from the end. Replace 'my/his/her' with the actual person immediately.[reference:15]
formulas.explanation: Sentences like 'my father's mother's only son' are designed to confuse you if read left-to-right. If you read right-to-left, it's just: 'mother's only son' (which is my father) -> 'my father' (which is me). Simple![reference:16]
formulas.example: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the brother of my father's only daughter.' Step 1: 'my father's only daughter' -> Since speaker is a woman, it's HER. Step 2: 'He is the brother of ME' -> He is my brother.[reference:17]
formulas.commonMistake: Starting from 'He is the brother...' and getting stuck. Always start from the deepest nested relation ('my father's...').

practiceProblems.question: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the son of my mother's only daughter.' How is the man related to the woman?
practiceProblems.explanation: Start from end: 'my mother's only daughter' = the woman herself (since she is female). So the man is the son of the woman.[reference:18]

practiceProblems.question: Pointing to a photo, a man said, 'I have no siblings. That man's son is my father's son.' Whose photo is it?
practiceProblems.explanation: 'my father's son' = the man himself (no siblings). So the man in the photo's son is the man himself, so the man in the photo is his father.[reference:19]

practiceProblems.question: Pointing to a girl, a boy said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the girl related to the boy?
practiceProblems.explanation: 'my grandfather's only son' = the boy's father. So the girl is the daughter of the boy's father, thus his sister.[reference:20]

practiceProblems.question: Pointing to a woman, a man said, 'She is the mother of my wife's only son.' How is the woman related to the man?
practiceProblems.explanation: 'my wife's only son' = the man's son. So the woman is the mother of his son, hence his wife.

practiceProblems.question: Pointing to a man, a lady said, 'He is the son of my father's only son.' (Lady is female). How is the man related to the lady?
practiceProblems.explanation: 'my father's only son' = the lady's brother (since she is female). So the man is the son of her brother, i.e., her nephew.[reference:21]

formulas.title: Coded Relations Chaining Rule
formulas.formula: Decode Symbol → Identify Gender → Chain Left-to-Right → Find End Relation.
formulas.whenToUse: When the question uses symbols like A + B, A - B, A * B, A / B.[reference:22][reference:23]
formulas.theTrick: Write the meaning of each symbol above the letters. Then, draw a quick line connecting them. Check the gender of the final person to ensure it matches the options.[reference:24]
formulas.explanation: It's like a math equation, but for family! If '+' means father, 'A + B' means A is the dad of B. If you have 'A + B - C', it means A is dad of B, and B is sister of C. So A is also the dad of C. Just chain them![reference:25]
formulas.example: P * Q + R. '*' means mother, '+' means brother. P is mother of Q. Q is brother of R. Therefore, P is the mother of R.[reference:26]
formulas.commonMistake: Forgetting to check the gender of the subject in the final answer. If the question asks 'How is P related to R?', and P is female, the answer must be 'Mother' or 'Sister', not 'Father' or 'Brother'.[reference:27]

practiceProblems.question: If A + B means A is father of B, A - B means A is sister of B. What is P + Q - R? (P related to R?)
practiceProblems.explanation: P+Q = P is father of Q; Q-R = Q is sister of R. So P is father of Q and Q is sister of R => P is father of R.[reference:28]

practiceProblems.question: If A * B means A is brother of B, A / B means A is mother of B. Then P * Q / R?
practiceProblems.explanation: P*Q = P is brother of Q; Q/R = Q is mother of R. So P is brother of Q, and Q is mother of R => P is uncle of R (maternal uncle).[reference:29]

practiceProblems.question: If A + B means A is sister of B, A - B means A is father of B. Then P + Q - R?
practiceProblems.explanation: P+Q = P is sister of Q; Q-R = Q is father of R. So P is sister of Q, and Q is father of R => P is aunt of R (paternal aunt).[reference:30]

practiceProblems.question: If A * B means A is husband of B, A / B means A is daughter of B. Then P * Q / R?
practiceProblems.explanation: P*Q = P is husband of Q; Q/R = Q is daughter of R. So P is husband of R's daughter => P is son-in-law of R.[reference:31]

practiceProblems.question: If A + B means A is mother of B, A - B means A is son of B. Then P + Q - R?
practiceProblems.explanation: P+Q = P is mother of Q; Q-R = Q is son of R. So P is mother of Q, and Q is son of R => P is wife of R (since R is father of Q).

formulas.title: The 'Only Son/Daughter' Identity Rule
formulas.formula: My father's only son (if I am male) = ME. My father's only daughter (if I am female) = ME.
formulas.whenToUse: When the phrase 'only son' or 'only daughter' appears in a pointing question.[reference:32]
formulas.theTrick: If the speaker says 'my father's only son' and the speaker is male, it's a reflex to replace it with 'ME'. If the speaker is female, it means 'MY BROTHER'.
formulas.explanation: Parents have children. If a father has ONLY ONE son, and the speaker is a boy, that boy MUST be the only son. If the speaker is a girl, her father's only son is her brother.[reference:33]
formulas.example: A man says, 'I have no brothers or sisters. That man's son is my father's son.' Step 1: 'my father's only son' (since I have no siblings) = ME. Step 2: 'That man's son is ME'. Step 3: I am the son of that man. So the man in the photo is my FATHER.[reference:34]
formulas.commonMistake: Assuming 'only son' always means 'ME'. If the speaker is a woman, 'my father's only son' is her BROTHER, not her!

practiceProblems.question: A man says, 'I have no brother. His father is my father's son.' Whose photo is it? (Man is speaker)
practiceProblems.explanation: 'my father's son' = the man himself (since he has no brother). So 'His father is me' => he is my son.

practiceProblems.question: A woman says, 'I have no sisters. His mother is my father's only daughter.' How is he related to the woman?
practiceProblems.explanation: 'my father's only daughter' = the woman herself (since she is female and no sisters). So 'His mother is me' => he is her son.

practiceProblems.question: A boy says, 'My father's only son is my brother.' Is that possible?
practiceProblems.answer: No, it's a contradiction; if the speaker is male, his father's only son is himself, not his brother.
practiceProblems.explanation: If the speaker is male, his father's only son is himself, so he cannot have a brother. So statement is false.

practiceProblems.question: A girl says, 'My mother's only daughter is my sister.' Is that possible?
practiceProblems.answer: No, if the speaker is female, her mother's only daughter is herself, so she cannot have a sister. So statement is false.
practiceProblems.explanation: If the speaker is female, her mother's only daughter is herself, so she cannot have a sister.

practiceProblems.question: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the only son of my father-in-law.' How is the man related to the woman?
practiceProblems.explanation: Woman's father-in-law = husband's father. The only son of husband's father = her husband (if he has no brothers). So the man is her husband.

formulas.title: In-Law & Spouse Generation Rule
formulas.formula: Spouse = Same Generation | In-laws (Parents/Siblings) = Same Generation as Spouse
formulas.theTrick: Marriage connects two people on the EXACT same horizontal level. Anyone related by marriage (in-laws) is also on that same level, unless they are the in-law's parents (who would be one level up).[reference:36]
formulas.explanation: Think of marriage as a bridge between two families at the same height. Your husband is on your level. His mother (your mother-in-law) is on your parents' level. His sister (your sister-in-law) is on your level.[reference:37][reference:38]
formulas.example: How is your wife's mother's only son related to you? Step 1: Wife's mother = Mother-in-law. Step 2: Her only son = Wife's brother. Step 3: Wife's brother = Brother-in-law. (All on the same generation level as you).[reference:39]
formulas.commonMistake: Thinking 'sister-in-law' is always the spouse's sister. It can ALSO be your own sibling's wife! Both are on your generation level.[reference:40]

practiceProblems.question: A is husband of B. B is sister of C. How is A related to C?
practiceProblems.explanation: A is married to B, B is C's sister, so A is C's brother-in-law (sister's husband).[reference:41]

practiceProblems.question: X is wife of Y. Y is brother of Z. How is X related to Z?
practiceProblems.explanation: X is married to Y, Y is Z's brother, so X is Z's sister-in-law (brother's wife).[reference:42]

practiceProblems.question: P is father of Q. Q is wife of R. How is P related to R?
practiceProblems.explanation: P is father of Q, Q is R's wife, so P is R's father-in-law (wife's father).[reference:43]

practiceProblems.question: M is mother of N. N is husband of O. How is M related to O?
practiceProblems.explanation: M is mother of N, N is O's husband, so M is O's mother-in-law (husband's mother).[reference:44]

practiceProblems.question: A is brother of B. B is wife of C. How is A related to C?
practiceProblems.explanation: A is B's brother, B is C's wife, so A is C's brother-in-law (wife's brother).[reference:45]

formulas.title: Maternal vs Paternal Distinction
formulas.formula: Paternal = Father's side (father's brother = paternal uncle). Maternal = Mother's side (mother's brother = maternal uncle).
formulas.whenToUse: When the question uses terms like 'maternal' or 'paternal' explicitly, or when you need to differentiate between relatives from father's and mother's side.[reference:46]
formulas.theTrick: Always identify the connecting parent: if the relation goes through the father, it's paternal; through the mother, it's maternal.[reference:47]
formulas.explanation: In many questions, the terms 'uncle' or 'grandfather' can be ambiguous. The words 'maternal' and 'paternal' clarify which parent's side. Paternal means from the father's side, maternal from the mother's side.[reference:48]
formulas.example: Your paternal grandfather is your father's father. Your maternal grandfather is your mother's father.[reference:49]
formulas.commonMistake: Confusing the two. Remember: Pater = Father, Mater = Mother.[reference:50]

practiceProblems.question: A's father's brother is A's what?
practiceProblems.explanation: Father's side is paternal; father's brother is uncle, so paternal uncle.[reference:51]

practiceProblems.question: A's mother's brother is A's what?
practiceProblems.explanation: Mother's side is maternal; mother's brother is uncle, so maternal uncle.[reference:52]

practiceProblems.question: A's father's father is A's what?
practiceProblems.explanation: Father's father is paternal grandfather.[reference:53]

practiceProblems.question: A's mother's mother is A's what?
practiceProblems.explanation: Mother's mother is maternal grandmother.[reference:54]

practiceProblems.question: A's father's sister's son is A's what? (A is male)
practiceProblems.explanation: Father's sister is paternal aunt; her son is A's cousin, specifically paternal cousin (since through father's side).[reference:55]

formulas.title: Generation Count Rule
formulas.formula: Same generation: Brother, Sister, Cousin | +1 generation: Father, Mother, Uncle, Aunt | +2 generation: Grandfather, Grandmother | -1 generation: Son, Daughter, Nephew, Niece | -2 generation: Grandson, Granddaughter[reference:56][reference:57]
formulas.whenToUse: When you need to determine the generation gap between two people in a family tree.[reference:58]
formulas.theTrick: Count the number of parent-child links between two people. One link = parent/child, two links = grandparent/grandchild, three links = great-grandparent/great-grandchild.[reference:59]
formulas.explanation: Every parent-child relationship adds one generation. If A is B's father (1 link), A is +1 generation. If A is B's grandfather (2 links), A is +2 generation.[reference:60]
formulas.example: D is E's grandson (E is +2 above D). F is D's son (F is -1 below D). So E is +3 above F = great-grandparent.[reference:61]
formulas.commonMistake: Forgetting to count the generation of the reference person correctly. Always set the reference person as generation 0.

practiceProblems.question: A is B's father. B is C's mother. How many generations above C is A?
practiceProblems.explanation: A→B is 1 link, B→C is 1 link. Total 2 links above C = grandfather.[reference:62]

practiceProblems.question: P is Q's parent. Q is R's sibling. R is S's parent. How many generations above S is P?
practiceProblems.explanation: P→Q is 1 link. Q and R are siblings (same generation). R→S is 1 link. Total 2 links above S = grandparent.[reference:63]

practiceProblems.question: X is Y's grandfather. Y is Z's father. How is X related to Z?
practiceProblems.explanation: X→Y is 2 links (grandfather). Y→Z is 1 link (father). Total 3 links = great-grandfather.[reference:64]

practiceProblems.question: M is N's son. N is O's daughter. How is M related to O?
practiceProblems.explanation: O→N is 1 link (daughter). N→M is 1 link (son). Total 2 links below O = grandson.

practiceProblems.question: R is S's niece. S is T's brother. How is R related to T?
practiceProblems.explanation: R is S's niece (S is sibling of R's parent). S and T are siblings. So T is also sibling of R's parent. Thus R is T's niece.[reference:65]

formulas.title: Cousin Relations Rule
formulas.formula: Cousin = Child of Uncle/Aunt (same generation as you) | First cousin = child of your parent's sibling[reference:66]
formulas.whenToUse: When the question involves 'cousin' or relations through uncle/aunt.
formulas.theTrick: Cousins are always in the same generation as you. Your parent's sibling's child is your cousin.[reference:67]
formulas.explanation: Your uncle/aunt is your parent's sibling. Their children are your cousins. They are in your generation (0).
formulas.example: Your father's brother's son is your cousin (paternal cousin). Your mother's sister's daughter is your cousin (maternal cousin).
formulas.commonMistake: Thinking cousins are in a different generation. They are ALWAYS in the same generation as you.

practiceProblems.question: A's father's brother's son is A's what?
practiceProblems.explanation: Father's brother is paternal uncle. His son is A's cousin.[reference:68]

practiceProblems.question: A's mother's sister's daughter is A's what?
practiceProblems.explanation: Mother's sister is maternal aunt. Her daughter is A's cousin.[reference:69]

practiceProblems.question: If A is B's cousin, and B is C's sibling, how is A related to C?
practiceProblems.explanation: A and B are cousins (same generation). B and C are siblings (same generation). So A and C are also cousins (same generation).

practiceProblems.question: A's father's sister's son is A's what?
practiceProblems.explanation: Father's sister is paternal aunt. Her son is A's cousin.[reference:70]

practiceProblems.question: If X is Y's cousin, and Y is Z's parent, how is X related to Z?
practiceProblems.answer: Cannot be determined (X is Y's generation, Z is Y's child, so X is Z's uncle/aunt if X is sibling of Y's parent)
practiceProblems.explanation: X and Y are same generation. Y is parent of Z. So X is in Y's generation, which is one above Z. If X is male, uncle; if female, aunt. Gender of X unknown, so cannot determine.

formulas.title: Chain Breaking Rule (My/His/Her)
formulas.formula: Break the chain at 'my', 'his', 'her' and replace with the actual person.
formulas.whenToUse: When the statement contains multiple possessive pronouns like 'my', 'his', 'her'.[reference:71]
formulas.theTrick: Identify the deepest nested relation first. Replace pronouns with actual people. Work from inside out.[reference:72]
formulas.explanation: Statements like 'my mother's husband's sister' can be broken down: 'my mother' = my mom, 'my mother's husband' = my father, 'my father's sister' = my aunt. Work from the innermost relation outward.[reference:73]
formulas.example: 'My mother's husband's only son' -> 'My mother's husband' = My father. 'My father's only son' = Me (if I'm male) or My brother.
formulas.commonMistake: Trying to solve the entire chain at once without breaking it down step by step.

practiceProblems.question: What is 'my mother's husband's sister' to me?
practiceProblems.explanation: My mother's husband = my father. My father's sister = my aunt.

practiceProblems.question: What is 'my father's wife's brother' to me?
practiceProblems.explanation: My father's wife = my mother. My mother's brother = my maternal uncle.

practiceProblems.question: What is 'my brother's wife's mother' to me?
practiceProblems.answer: Cannot be determined (could be mother-in-law of brother)
practiceProblems.explanation: My brother's wife = sister-in-law. Her mother = my brother's mother-in-law. Not directly related to me by blood.

practiceProblems.question: What is 'my sister's husband's father' to me?
practiceProblems.explanation: My sister's husband = brother-in-law. His father = my sister's father-in-law. Not directly related to me.

practiceProblems.question: What is 'my mother's mother's only daughter' to me?
practiceProblems.explanation: My mother's mother = grandmother. Her only daughter = my mother (if she has no sisters).

formulas.title: Gender Unknown Rule
formulas.formula: Never assume gender unless explicitly stated. If gender is unknown, the answer may be 'Cannot be determined'.
formulas.whenToUse: When the question does not specify the gender of a person.[reference:74]
formulas.theTrick: If the question says 'sibling' or 'child' without specifying gender, leave the box unmarked until a later statement confirms it.[reference:75]
formulas.explanation: Many students make the mistake of assuming 'child' is a son or 'sibling' is a brother. Always check if gender is given. If not, the answer might be 'Cannot be determined'.
formulas.example: A is the child of B. But B is not the father of A. Who is B? Step 1: A is the child of B. So B is a parent. Step 2: B is NOT the father. Step 3: If B is a parent but not the father, B MUST be the MOTHER.

practiceProblems.question: A is the child of B. B is not the father of A. Who is B?
practiceProblems.explanation: If B is a parent but not the father, B must be the mother.

practiceProblems.question: X is the sibling of Y. Y is the father of Z. How is X related to Z?
practiceProblems.answer: Cannot be determined (X could be uncle or aunt depending on gender)
practiceProblems.explanation: X is Y's sibling. Y is Z's father. So X is Z's uncle if male, aunt if female. Gender of X is unknown.

practiceProblems.question: P is the parent of Q. Q is the parent of R. How is P related to R?
practiceProblems.explanation: P is parent of Q, Q is parent of R. So P is grandparent of R. Gender of P is unknown (could be grandfather or grandmother).

practiceProblems.question: A is B's sibling. C is B's child. How is A related to C?
practiceProblems.answer: Uncle or Aunt (gender of A unknown)
practiceProblems.explanation: A is sibling of B, B is parent of C. So A is C's uncle if male, aunt if female.

practiceProblems.question: M is N's spouse. O is N's child. How is M related to O?
practiceProblems.answer: Parent (step-parent or biological, gender of M unknown)
practiceProblems.explanation: M is N's spouse, O is N's child. M is O's parent (step-parent if not biological, or biological parent if M is the other parent). Gender of M unknown.

family-tree-symbols.q: If A is the father of B, and B is the sister of C, what is the relation of A to C?
s: Draw A(+) at the top of the tree.
Draw B(-) and C(?) on the same horizontal row, connected by a horizontal line (siblings).
Draw a vertical line from A down to both B and C, showing A is the parent of both.
Since A is male (father) and is the parent of C, A is the Father of C.

family-tree-symbols.q: If X is the mother of Y, and Y is the brother of Z, what is X to Z?
s: Draw X(-) at the top.
Draw Y(+) and Z(?) on the same row as siblings (horizontal line).
Connect X vertically to both Y and Z.
X is the mother of both, so X is Mother of Z.

family-tree-symbols.q: If P is husband of Q, and Q is sister of R, how is P related to R?
s: Draw P(+) and Q(-) on the same horizontal row connected by a double line (=) representing marriage.
Draw Q and R as siblings on the same row (horizontal line).
P is the husband of Q. Since Q is R's sister, P is R's brother-in-law.

family-tree-symbols.q: If A is son of B, B is daughter of C, then A is C's?
s: C is the parent of B (since B is daughter of C).
B is the parent of A (since A is son of B).
This makes C the grandparent of A. Since A is male (son), C is A's Grandfather or Grandmother. Without gender of C, the relation is Grandson.

family-tree-symbols.q: If M is father of N, N is mother of O, then M is O's?
s: M is father of N.
N is mother of O.
M is the father of O's mother, so M is O's Grandfather.

family-tree-symbols.q: If A is B's sister, C is B's mother, and D is C's father, how is D related to A?
s: A, B, and C form a chain. C is B's mother, and A and B are siblings, so C is also A's mother.
D is C's father, so D is A's mother's father, i.e., D is A's Grandfather.

family-tree-symbols.q: If P is Q's brother, R is Q's sister, and S is R's son, how is P related to S?
s: P, Q, and R are siblings (same generation).
R is S's mother.
P is the brother of S's mother, so P is S's Uncle.

family-tree-symbols.q: If M is N's sister, O is N's brother, and P is O's daughter, how is M related to P?
s: M, N, and O are siblings.
O is P's father.
M is the sister of P's father, so M is P's Aunt.

family-tree-symbols.q: If A is B's wife, C is A's brother, and D is C's mother, how is D related to B?
s: C is A's brother, so D is also A's mother.
A is B's wife.
B's wife's mother is B's Mother-in-law.

family-tree-symbols.q: If X is Y's husband, Z is X's sister, and W is Z's mother, how is W related to Y?
s: Z is X's sister, so W is also X's mother.
X is Y's husband.
Y's husband's mother is Y's Mother-in-law.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the son of my mother's only daughter.' How is the man related to the woman?
s: Start from the end: 'my mother's only daughter' = the woman herself (since she is female and is the only daughter).
Replace it: 'He is the son of ME'.
So the man is the Son of the woman.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a photo, a man said, 'I have no siblings. That man's son is my father's son.' Whose photo is it?
s: Start from the end: 'my father's son' = the man himself (since he has no siblings).
Replace it: 'That man's son is ME'.
If his son is me, the man in the photo is my Father.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a girl, a boy said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the girl related to the boy?
s: Start from the end: 'my grandfather's only son' = the boy's father (since the boy is male).
Replace it: 'She is the daughter of my father'.
Father's daughter is the boy's Sister.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a woman, a man said, 'She is the mother of my wife's only son.' How is the woman related to the man?
s: Start from the end: 'my wife's only son' = the man's son.
Replace it: 'She is the mother of my son'.
The mother of the man's son is his Wife.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a man, a lady said, 'He is the son of my father's only son.' (Lady is female). How is the man related to the lady?
s: Start from the end: 'my father's only son' = the lady's brother (since she is female).
Replace it: 'He is the son of my brother'.
Brother's son is the lady's Nephew.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a photograph, a woman said, 'This man's son is my husband.' How is the man in the photo related to the woman?
s: 'This man's son' = her husband.
Therefore, the man in the photo is the father of her husband.
Husband's father is Father-in-law.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a boy, a woman said, 'He is the son of my mother's only daughter.' (Woman is female). How is the boy related?
s: End: 'my mother's only daughter' = the woman herself.
'He is the son of ME'.
So the boy is her Son.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my father.' How is the woman related to the man?
s: End: 'my father's only daughter' = the woman herself.
'His mother is ME'.
So the woman is the man's Mother.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a man, a lady said, 'He is the brother of my father's only daughter.' (Lady is female). How is the man related?
s: End: 'my father's only daughter' = the lady herself.
'He is the brother of ME'.
So the man is her Brother.

pointing-backward-rule.q: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the father of my son's mother.' How is the man related to the woman?
s: End: 'my son's mother' = the woman herself.
'He is the father of ME'.
So the man is her Father.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A + B means A is father of B, A - B means A is sister of B. What is P + Q - R? (P related to R?)
s: P + Q => P is father of Q.
Q - R => Q is sister of R.
Since P is father of Q, and Q is sister of R, P is also the father of R.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A * B means A is brother of B, A / B means A is mother of B. Then P * Q / R?
s: P * Q => P is brother of Q.
Q / R => Q is mother of R.
P is the brother of R's mother, so P is R's Maternal Uncle.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A + B means A is sister of B, A - B means A is father of B. Then P + Q - R?
s: P + Q => P is sister of Q.
Q - R => Q is father of R.
P is the sister of R's father, so P is R's Paternal Aunt.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A * B means A is husband of B, A / B means A is daughter of B. Then P * Q / R?
s: P * Q => P is husband of Q.
Q / R => Q is daughter of R.
P is the husband of R's daughter, so P is R's Son-in-law.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A + B means A is mother of B, A - B means A is son of B. Then P + Q - R?
s: P + Q => P is mother of Q.
Q - R => Q is son of R.
Since Q is son of R, R is father of Q. P is mother of Q. So P is the wife of R.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A $ B means A is father of B, A # B means A is sister of B, A @ B means A is mother of B. Find F @ D $ K # H. How is H related to F?
s: F @ D => F is mother of D.
D $ K => D is father of K.
K # H => K is sister of H.
F is grandmother of K and H. H's gender is unknown.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A + B means A is brother of B, A - B means A is wife of B. Then P - Q + R?
s: P - Q => P is wife of Q.
Q + R => Q is brother of R.
P is the wife of R's brother, so P is R's Sister-in-law.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A * B means A is mother of B, A / B means A is son of B. Then P * Q / R?
s: P * Q => P is mother of Q.
Q / R => Q is son of R.
P is the wife of R (since R is father of Q). So P is R's Wife.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A # B means A is daughter of B, A $ B means A is father of B. Then P # Q $ R?
s: P # Q => P is daughter of Q.
Q $ R => Q is father of R.
P and R are siblings (both children of Q). P is female, so P is R's Sister.

coded-relation-chain.q: If A & B means A is sister of B, A % B means A is brother of B. Then P & Q % R?
s: P & Q => P is sister of Q.
Q % R => Q is brother of R.
P, Q, and R are siblings. P is female, so P is R's Sister.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: A man says, 'I have no brother. His father is my father's son.' Whose photo is it? (Man is speaker)
s: 'my father's son' = the man himself (since he has no brother).
'His father is ME'.
So the person in the photo is his Son.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: A woman says, 'I have no sisters. His mother is my father's only daughter.' How is he related to the woman?
s: 'my father's only daughter' = the woman herself (since she is female and has no sisters).
'His mother is ME'.
So he is her Son.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: A boy says, 'My father's only son is my brother.' Is that possible?
s: If the speaker is male, his father's only son is HIMSELF.
He cannot have a brother if he is the only son.
So the statement is FALSE.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: A girl says, 'My mother's only daughter is my sister.' Is that possible?
s: If the speaker is female, her mother's only daughter is HERSELF.
She cannot have a sister if she is the only daughter.
So the statement is FALSE.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the only son of my father-in-law.' How is the man related to the woman?
s: Woman's father-in-law = husband's father.
The only son of husband's father = her husband (assuming no brothers).
So the man is her Husband.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: A man says, 'That man's sister is my daughter. I have no siblings.' How is the man in the photo related?
s: 'That man's sister' = my daughter.
If his sister is my daughter, he is my Son.
So the man in the photo is his Son.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: 'Your father's only son is my father.' How are you related to me?
s: 'Your father's only son' = YOU (if male).
YOU are my father.
So I am your son/daughter. You are my Father.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: 'My father's only daughter is your mother.' How am I related to you? (Speaker is female)
s: 'My father's only daughter' = ME (since I am female).
I am your mother.
So I am your Mother.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: A woman says, 'I have no sisters. His sister is my father's only daughter.' How is he related?
s: 'my father's only daughter' = the woman herself.
His sister is ME.
So he is her Brother.

only-son-daughter-rule.q: A man says, 'I have no siblings. That woman's son is my father's son.' Whose photo?
s: 'my father's son' = the man himself.
'That woman's son' = ME.
So that woman is his Mother.

in-law-generation-rule.q: A is husband of B. B is sister of C. How is A related to C?
s: A is married to B (same generation).
B is C's sister (same generation).
A is C's brother-in-law (sister's husband).

in-law-generation-rule.q: X is wife of Y. Y is brother of Z. How is X related to Z?
s: X is married to Y (same generation).
Y is Z's brother (same generation).
X is Z's sister-in-law (brother's wife).

in-law-generation-rule.q: P is father of Q. Q is wife of R. How is P related to R?
s: P is father of Q (Q is +1 generation below P).
Q is R's wife (same generation).
P is R's father-in-law (wife's father).

in-law-generation-rule.q: M is mother of N. N is husband of O. How is M related to O?
s: M is mother of N.
N is O's husband.
M is O's mother-in-law (husband's mother).

in-law-generation-rule.q: A is brother of B. B is wife of C. How is A related to C?
s: A is B's brother.
B is C's wife.
A is C's brother-in-law (wife's brother).

in-law-generation-rule.q: P is sister of Q. Q is husband of R. How is P related to R?
s: P is Q's sister.
Q is R's husband.
P is R's sister-in-law (husband's sister).

in-law-generation-rule.q: A is father of B. B is husband of C. How is A related to C?
s: A is father of B.
B is C's husband.
A is C's father-in-law (husband's father).

in-law-generation-rule.q: X is mother of Y. Y is wife of Z. How is X related to Z?
s: X is mother of Y.
Y is Z's wife.
X is Z's mother-in-law (wife's mother).

in-law-generation-rule.q: A is son of B. B is wife of C. How is A related to C?
s: A is son of B.
B is wife of C.
A is son of C's wife => C is the father, so A is C's Son.

in-law-generation-rule.q: X is daughter of Y. Y is husband of Z. How is X related to Z?
s: X is daughter of Y.
Y is Z's husband.
Z is the wife of X's father, so Z is X's Mother.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's father's brother is A's what?
s: Father's side = Paternal.
Father's brother = Uncle.
So A's father's brother is A's Paternal Uncle.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's mother's brother is A's what?
s: Mother's side = Maternal.
Mother's brother = Uncle.
So A's mother's brother is A's Maternal Uncle.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's father's father is A's what?
s: Father's side = Paternal.
Father's father = Grandfather.
So A's father's father is A's Paternal Grandfather.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's mother's mother is A's what?
s: Mother's side = Maternal.
Mother's mother = Grandmother.
So A's mother's mother is A's Maternal Grandmother.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's father's sister's son is A's what? (A is male)
s: Father's side = Paternal.
Father's sister = Paternal Aunt.
Her son = A's cousin (paternal cousin).

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's mother's sister's daughter is A's what?
s: Mother's side = Maternal.
Mother's sister = Maternal Aunt.
Her daughter = A's cousin (maternal cousin).

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's paternal grandmother is which parent's mother?
s: Paternal = Father's side.
Grandmother = mother of a parent.
So paternal grandmother = Father's mother.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's maternal grandfather is which parent's father?
s: Maternal = Mother's side.
Grandfather = father of a parent.
So maternal grandfather = Mother's father.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's father's mother's son (who is not A's father) is A's what?
s: Father's mother = Paternal Grandmother.
Her son = A's father's brother (since it's not A's father).
So he is A's Paternal Uncle.

maternal-paternal-distinction.q: A's mother's father's daughter (who is not A's mother) is A's what?
s: Mother's father = Maternal Grandfather.
His daughter = A's mother's sister (since it's not A's mother).
So she is A's Maternal Aunt.

generation-count.q: A is B's father. B is C's mother. How many generations above C is A?
s: A -> B is 1 generation (father-child).
B -> C is 1 generation (mother-child).
Total 2 generations above C.
So A is C's Grandfather.

generation-count.q: P is Q's parent. Q is R's sibling. R is S's parent. How many generations above S is P?
s: P -> Q is 1 link.
Q and R are siblings (same generation, 0 links).
R -> S is 1 link.
Total 2 links above S. P is S's Grandparent.

generation-count.q: X is Y's grandfather. Y is Z's father. How is X related to Z?
s: X -> Y is 2 links (grandparent).
Y -> Z is 1 link (parent-child).
Total 3 links. X is Z's Great-grandfather.

generation-count.q: M is N's son. N is O's daughter. How is M related to O?
s: O -> N is 1 link (daughter).
N -> M is 1 link (son).
Total 2 links below O. M is O's Grandson.

generation-count.q: R is S's niece. S is T's brother. How is R related to T?
s: R is S's niece => S is sibling of R's parent.
S and T are siblings => T is also sibling of R's parent.
So T is the sibling of R's parent, making R the niece of T as well.

generation-count.q: A is B's uncle. B is C's father. How is A related to C?
s: A is B's uncle => A is sibling of B's parent.
B is C's father.
So A is the sibling of C's grandfather (or grandmother). A is C's Granduncle.

generation-count.q: If X is Y's grandmother, and Y is Z's aunt, how is X related to Z?
s: X -> Y is 2 links (grandmother).
Y -> Z: Y is Z's aunt, so Y is sibling of Z's parent. 0 links, same generation.
X is Z's Great-grandparent (2 links above Z).

generation-count.q: P is Q's brother. Q is R's mother. How is P related to R?
s: P and Q are siblings (same generation).
Q is R's mother (Q is 1 generation above R).
P is also 1 generation above R. P is R's Uncle.

generation-count.q: A is B's sister. B is C's son. How is A related to C?
s: A and B are siblings.
B is C's son => C is parent of B.
So C is also parent of A. A is C's Daughter.

generation-count.q: X is Y's wife. Y is Z's grandfather. How is X related to Z?
s: Y is Z's grandfather (2 links above).
X is Y's wife (same generation).
So X is also 2 links above Z. X is Z's Great-grandmother.

cousin-relations.q: A's father's brother's son is A's what?
s: Father's brother = Paternal Uncle.
Uncle's son = Cousin.
So the relation is Cousin (paternal cousin).

cousin-relations.q: A's mother's sister's daughter is A's what?
s: Mother's sister = Maternal Aunt.
Aunt's daughter = Cousin.
So the relation is Cousin (maternal cousin).

cousin-relations.q: If A is B's cousin, and B is C's sibling, how is A related to C?
s: A and B are cousins (same generation).
B and C are siblings (same generation).
So A and C are also cousins (same generation).

cousin-relations.q: A's father's sister's son is A's what?
s: Father's sister = Paternal Aunt.
Aunt's son = Cousin.
So the relation is Cousin.

cousin-relations.q: If X is Y's cousin, and Y is Z's parent, how is X related to Z?
s: X and Y are same generation.
Y is parent of Z (Y is 1 generation above Z).
X is also 1 generation above Z (since X is same generation as Y).
X is Z's uncle (if male) or aunt (if female). Gender of X unknown.

cousin-relations.q: A's mother's brother's daughter is A's what?
s: Mother's brother = Maternal Uncle.
Uncle's daughter = Cousin.
So the relation is Cousin.

cousin-relations.q: P is Q's cousin. R is P's sibling. How is R related to Q?
s: P and Q are cousins.
R is P's sibling (same generation).
So R is also in the same generation as Q. R is Q's cousin as well.

cousin-relations.q: A is B's cousin. C is B's parent. How is A related to C?
s: A is B's cousin => A's parent is sibling of B's parent.
C is B's parent.
A is the child of C's sibling, so A is C's Niece/Nephew. Gender of A unknown.

cousin-relations.q: Your father's brother's daughter is your?
s: Father's brother = Uncle.
Uncle's daughter = Cousin.
So she is your Cousin.

cousin-relations.q: Your mother's sister's son is your?
s: Mother's sister = Aunt.
Aunt's son = Cousin.
So he is your Cousin.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my mother's husband's sister' to me?
s: Break at 'my mother's husband' => My father.
Now 'my father's sister' => My aunt.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my father's wife's brother' to me?
s: 'my father's wife' => My mother.
'my mother's brother' => My maternal uncle.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my brother's wife's mother' to me?
s: 'my brother's wife' => My sister-in-law.
'my sister-in-law's mother' => My brother's mother-in-law.
No direct blood relation to me.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my sister's husband's father' to me?
s: 'my sister's husband' => My brother-in-law.
'my brother-in-law's father' => My sister's father-in-law.
No direct blood relation to me.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my mother's mother's only daughter' to me?
s: 'my mother's mother' => My grandmother.
'my grandmother's only daughter' => My mother (if she has no sisters).
So she is my Mother.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my father's brother's wife' to me?
s: 'my father's brother' => My paternal uncle.
'my uncle's wife' => My aunt (paternal aunt by marriage).

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my son's father's mother' to me?
s: 'my son's father' => My husband (if I am female).
'my husband's mother' => My mother-in-law.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my wife's father's only son' to me?
s: 'my wife's father' => My father-in-law.
'my father-in-law's only son' => My wife's brother (if no brothers).
Wife's brother => My brother-in-law.

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my uncle's daughter's brother' to me?
s: 'my uncle's daughter' => My cousin.
'my cousin's brother' => Also my cousin (since they are siblings).

chain-breaking.q: What is 'my grandmother's only son's daughter' to me?
s: 'my grandmother's only son' => My father.
'my father's daughter' => My sister.

gender-unknown.q: A is the child of B. B is not the father of A. Who is B?
s: A is the child of B => B is a parent.
B is NOT the father.
If B is a parent but not the father, B must be the mother.

gender-unknown.q: X is the sibling of Y. Y is the father of Z. How is X related to Z?
s: X is Y's sibling (same generation).
Y is Z's father (Y is 1 generation above Z).
X is also 1 generation above Z.
Gender of X is unknown => X is Uncle (if male) or Aunt (if female).

gender-unknown.q: P is the parent of Q. Q is the parent of R. How is P related to R?
s: P is parent of Q.
Q is parent of R.
P is grandparent of R.
Gender of P is unknown => Grandfather or Grandmother.

gender-unknown.q: A is B's sibling. C is B's child. How is A related to C?
s: A is sibling of B.
B is parent of C.
A is in the generation above C.
Gender of A unknown => Uncle or Aunt.

gender-unknown.q: M is N's spouse. O is N's child. How is M related to O?
s: M is N's spouse.
O is N's child.
M is the other parent of O (or step-parent).
Gender of M unknown => Father or Mother.

gender-unknown.q: A is the father of B. B is the parent of C. How is A related to C?
s: A is father of B (A is male, 1 generation above B).
B is parent of C (B is 1 generation above C).
A is 2 generations above C.
A is male, so A is Grandfather.

gender-unknown.q: X is the mother of Y. Y is the sibling of Z. How is X related to Z?
s: X is mother of Y.
Y and Z are siblings.
X is also mother of Z.
X is female, so X is Mother.

gender-unknown.q: P is the son of Q. R is the daughter of P. How is Q related to R?
s: P is son of Q => Q is parent of P.
R is daughter of P => P is parent of R.
Q is grandparent of R. Gender of Q unknown.

gender-unknown.q: A is B's brother. C is B's sister. How is A related to C?
s: A and B are siblings (brother-sister).
C and B are siblings (sister-sister).
Therefore, A and C are also siblings.
A is male, so A is C's Brother.

gender-unknown.q: M is N's uncle. O is N's sibling. How is M related to O?
s: M is N's uncle (1 generation above N).
O is N's sibling (same generation as N).
M is also 1 generation above O.
M is male, so M is O's Uncle.

mcqs.q: Introducing a woman, a man said, 'Her husband is the only son of my father'. How is that woman related to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The man's father has only one son, who is the man himself. Step 2: That son is the husband of the woman. Step 3: Therefore, the woman is the wife of the man.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Husband) is incorrect gender. Option B (Sister) would mean she is his sibling. Option D (Mother) would mean she is his parent.
mcqs.trap: Assuming 'only son' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: Pointing towards a person, a woman said to a man, 'His father is the only son of your mother'. How is the man related to that person?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The man's mother has only one son, who is the man himself. Step 2: That son is the father of that person. Step 3: Therefore, the man is the father of that person.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Brother) would mean same generation. Option C (Son) would reverse the relationship. Option D (Husband) is not supported.

mcqs.q: X and Y are the children of Z. If Z is the father of X but Y is not the son of Z, then what is the relationship between Y and Z?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: X and Y are both children of Z. Step 2: Z is the father of X, so Z is male. Step 3: Y is not the son of Z. Since Y is a child of Z and not a son, Y must be the daughter of Z.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Sister and Brother) would mean Y is sibling of Z. Option C (Niece and Uncle) would mean Y is not a child. Option D is incorrect as data is sufficient.
mcqs.trap: Assuming Y is male because X is mentioned first.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a man tells his friend, 'She is the daughter of the only son of my father's wife'. How is the girl in the photograph related to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My father's wife' refers to the man's mother. Step 2: 'The only son of my father's wife' refers to the man himself. Step 3: 'The daughter of the only son...' means the daughter of the man himself. Therefore, the girl is the man's daughter.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Cousin) would mean she is uncle/aunt's child. Option C (Niece) would mean she is brother/sister's daughter. Option D (Sister) would mean same generation.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a lady, Suresh said, 'She is the daughter of the woman, who is the mother of the husband of my mother'. What is the relation of the lady to Suresh?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My mother' refers to Suresh's mother. Step 2: 'Husband of my mother' is Suresh's father. Step 3: 'Mother of the husband of my mother' is Suresh's grandmother. Step 4: 'Daughter of the woman (grandmother)' is the sister of Suresh's father. Step 5: The sister of one's father is known as an Aunt.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Granddaughter) is wrong generation. Option C (Cousin) is same generation. Option D (Sister) would mean she is his sibling.
mcqs.trap: Confusing the chain of 'mother of husband of my mother'.

mcqs.q: A girl introduced a boy as the son of the daughter of the father of her uncle. The boy is the girl's:
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The father of her uncle is her grandfather. Step 2: The daughter of her grandfather is her father's sister (aunt) or her mother. Step 3: The son of her mother is her brother. Therefore, the boy is the girl's brother.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Son) would mean she is his mother. Option C (Uncle) would mean he is her parent's sibling. Option D (Son-in-law) is marriage relation.
mcqs.trap: Assuming the daughter is the aunt instead of the mother.

mcqs.q: Introducing a man to her husband, a woman said, 'His brother's father is the only son of my grandfather.' How is the woman related to this man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'Only son of my grandfather' refers to the woman's father. Step 2: The man's brother's father is the same as the man's father. Step 3: Man's father = Woman's father. Step 4: This implies that the man and the woman are siblings. Therefore, the woman is the sister of the man.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would mean she is his parent. Option B (Aunt) would mean she is his parent's sibling. Option D (Daughter) would reverse the relationship.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'only son' refers to the man himself.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a lady, a girl said, 'She is the daughter-in-law of the grandmother of my father's only son'. How is the lady related to the girl?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My father's only son' is the girl's brother. Step 2: 'Grandmother of my brother' is the girl's grandmother. Step 3: 'Daughter-in-law of my grandmother' is the mother of the only son (or daughter-in-law). Since it's the only son's wife, it refers to the girl's mother. Therefore, the lady is the girl's mother.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Sister-in-law) would be brother's wife. Option C (Aunt) would be parent's sibling. Option D (Mother-in-law) is spouse's mother.

mcqs.q: Introducing a man, a woman said, 'He is the only son of my mother's mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My mother's mother' refers to the woman's grandmother. Step 2: 'The only son of my grandmother' refers to the woman's maternal uncle. Step 3: Since the man is the maternal uncle of the woman, the woman is the man's niece.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would mean she is his parent. Option B (Aunt) would mean she is his parent's sister. Option C (Sister) would mean same generation.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'only son' refers to the woman's father.

mcqs.q: If Neena says, 'Anita's father Raman is the only son of my father-in-law Mahipal', then how is Bindu, who is the sister of Anita, related to Mahipal?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Only son of Neena's father-in-law Mahipal is Neena's husband. Step 2: So, Raman is Neena's husband. Step 3: Anita and Bindu are Raman's daughters. Step 4: Thus, Bindu is the granddaughter of Mahipal, which is not listed as an option.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Wife) is incorrect. Option B (Daughter) would mean Bindu is Mahipal's child. Option C (Niece) would mean Bindu is Mahipal's sibling's child.
mcqs.trap: Forgetting that 'granddaughter' is not listed as an option.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a man on the stage, Rita said, 'He is the brother of the daughter of the wife of my husband.' How is the man on the stage related to Rita?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'Wife of my husband' is Rita herself. Step 2: 'Daughter of the wife of my husband' is Rita's daughter. Step 3: 'Brother of the daughter' is Rita's son. So, the man is Rita's son.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Husband) is wrong generation. Option B (Nephew) would be sibling's son. Option D (Cousin) is same generation.

mcqs.q: A woman introduces a man as the son of the brother of her mother. How is the man related to the woman?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Brother of mother = Maternal uncle. Step 2: Uncle's son = Cousin. Therefore, the man is the woman's cousin.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Son) is wrong generation. Option B (Grandson) is two generations below. Option D (Uncle) is parent's generation.

mcqs.q: Pointing towards a person, a man said to a woman, 'His mother is the only daughter of your father.' How is the woman related to that person?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The only daughter of the woman's father is the woman herself. Step 2: So, the person's mother is the woman. Step 3: Therefore, the woman is the person's mother.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Sister) would mean same generation. Option C (Daughter) would reverse the relationship. Option D (Wife) is not supported.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'only daughter' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: A girl introduced a boy as the son of the daughter of the father of her uncle. The boy is girl's:
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Daughter of uncle's father = Uncle's sister = Mother. Step 2: Mother's son = Brother. Therefore, the boy is the girl's brother.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Son) is wrong generation. Option C (Uncle) is parent's generation. Option D (Nephew) is sibling's son.
mcqs.trap: Assuming the daughter is the aunt instead of the mother.

mcqs.q: Rahul told Anand, 'Yesterday I defeated the only brother of the daughter of my grandmother.' Whom did Rahul defeat?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Daughter of grandmother = Aunt. Step 2: Aunt's only brother = Father. Therefore, Rahul defeated his father.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Son) is wrong generation. Option B (Brother) is same generation. Option C (Cousin) is same generation.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'daughter of grandmother' is the mother.

mcqs.q: Pointing to an old man, Kunal said, 'His son is my son's uncle.' How is the old man related to Kunal?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Kunal's son's uncle is Kunal's brother. Step 2: So, the old man's son is Kunal's brother. Step 3: Therefore, the old man is Kunal's father.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Uncle) is parent's sibling. Option B (Grandfather) is two generations above. Option D (Brother) is same generation.

mcqs.q: Aditya is Bhavi's brother, Bharat is Jayant's father. Ella is Bhavi's mother. Aditya and Jayant are brothers. What is Ella's relationship with Bharat?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Aditya and Jayant are brothers. Step 2: Aditya is Bhavi's brother, so Aditya, Jayant, and Bhavi are siblings. Step 3: Ella is Bhavi's mother, so Ella is also mother of Aditya and Jayant. Step 4: Bharat is Jayant's father. Since Ella is Jayant's mother and Bharat is Jayant's father, Ella is Bharat's wife.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Sister) is same generation. Option B (Mother) would mean Bharat is her child. Option C (Daughter) would mean Bharat is her parent.
mcqs.trap: Missing that Ella is mother of all three siblings.

mcqs.q: A man introduced the boy coming with him as 'He is son of the father of my wife's daughter'. What relation did the boy bear to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My wife's daughter' is the man's daughter (or step-daughter). Step 2: 'Father of my wife's daughter' is the man himself (if biological father). Step 3: 'Son of the father' means the son of the man. Therefore, the boy is the man's son.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Son-in-law) is daughter's husband. Option C (Brother) is same generation. Option D (Father) is wrong generation.
mcqs.trap: Assuming 'wife's daughter' is from a previous marriage.

mcqs.q: A and B are two brothers. C is sister of B. D is sister of E. E is son of A. Who is D's uncle?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: A and B are brothers. C is sister of B, so A, B, and C are siblings. Step 2: E is son of A. Step 3: D is sister of E, so D is also child of A. Step 4: D's uncle is the brother of D's parent (A). Since B is A's brother, B is D's uncle.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (D) is the person herself. Option B (E) is D's brother. Option C (C) is D's aunt (female).
mcqs.trap: Assuming D's uncle is someone other than B.

mcqs.exp: Step 1: Gopi is brother of Akhil. Step 2: Akhil is uncle of Vijay, so Vijay is nephew of Akhil. Step 3: Therefore, Vijay is also nephew of Gopi (since Gopi is Akhil's brother). mcqs.q: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'He is the only son of my mother's mother'. How is the woman related to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The man is the only son of the woman's grandmother. Step 2: That makes the man the woman's maternal uncle. Step 3: Therefore, the woman is the man's niece.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Aunt) would mean she is his parent's sister. Option B (Daughter) is wrong generation. Option D (Sister) is same generation.
mcqs.trap: Thinking the man is the woman's father.

mcqs.q: Pointing to Suman, Amit said, 'He is my sister's only brother's son'. How is Suman related to Amit?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My sister's only brother' is Amit himself. Step 2: 'My sister's only brother's son' is Amit's son. Step 3: Therefore, Suman is Amit's son.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Grandson) is two generations below. Option C (Nephew) would be sister's son. Option D is incorrect as data is sufficient.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'only brother' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a woman said, 'She is the only daughter of my mother's father.' How is the woman related to the person in the photograph?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My mother's father' is the woman's maternal grandfather. Step 2: 'The only daughter of my mother's father' is the woman's mother. Step 3: So, the person in the photograph is the woman's mother. Therefore, the woman is the daughter of the person in the photograph.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would reverse the relationship. Option B (Grandmother) is wrong generation. Option D is incorrect as data is sufficient.

mcqs.q: A and B are brothers. C and D are sisters. A's son is D's brother. How is B related to C?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: A's son is D's brother, so A's son and D are siblings. Step 2: Since C and D are sisters, C is also sibling of A's son. Step 3: Therefore, A is the parent of C and D. Step 4: A and B are brothers, so B is the brother of A. Step 5: Since A is the parent of C, B is the uncle of C. Wait, let me re-read: 'How is B related to C?' If A is the father of C and D (since A's son is D's brother), then B, being A's brother, is C's uncle. But the options don't have 'Uncle'. Let me re-evaluate. Actually, if A's son is D's brother, then D is also A's child. Since C and D are sisters, C is also A's child. A and B are brothers. So B is the brother of C's father. That makes B the uncle of C.
