mcqs.elimination: Option A (Father) would mean B is C's parent. Option B (Brother) would mean B is C's sibling. Option D (Nephew) would mean B is C's sibling's son.
mcqs.trap: Missing that C and D are sisters to A's son.

mcqs.q: A is the mother of B and C. D is the husband of C. How is A related to D?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: A is the mother of C. Step 2: D is the husband of C. Step 3: A is the mother-in-law of D (since A is C's mother, and D is C's husband, A is D's mother-in-law).
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would mean D is A's child. Option C (Aunt) is wrong. Option D (Sister) is same generation.

mcqs.q: A and B both are children of C. If C is the mother of A, A is the son of C but B is not the daughter of C, how are A and B mutually related?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: A and B are both children of C. Step 2: A is the son of C, so A is male. Step 3: B is not the daughter of C. Since B is a child of C and not a daughter, B must be a son. Step 4: Therefore, A and B are brothers.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Brother and Sister) would mean B is female. Option C (Sister and Sister) would mean both are female. Option D is incorrect.
mcqs.trap: Assuming 'not daughter' means B is female.

mcqs.q: If A + B means A is the mother of B; A x B means A is the father of B; A $ B means A is the brother of B and A @ B means A is the sister of B, then which of the following means P is the son of Q?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: We need P to be the son of Q, so Q must be the parent of P (either father or mother). Step 2: Option D: Q x R = Q is the father of R. R $ P = R is the brother of P. P $ N = P is the brother of N. Step 3: Since R is the brother of P, Q (father of R) is also the father of P. So P is the son of Q. Step 4: Verify other options: A gives Q as mother of R, R as sister of P, so Q is mother of P as well (P is child of Q). Actually A: Q + R = Q is mother of R. R @ P = R is sister of P. So Q is mother of P as well. P is child of Q. But we need P to be son (male). In A, P's gender is not specified (could be son or daughter). In D, P $ N means P is brother of N, so P is male. Therefore D is correct.
mcqs.elimination: Option A gives P's gender unknown. Option B and C have similar issues.
mcqs.trap: Forgetting to check the gender of P.

mcqs.q: A has 3 children. B is the brother of C and C is the sister of D. E who is the wife of A is the mother of D. There is only one daughter of the husband of E. What is the relation between D and B?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: E is the wife of A, so A is the husband of E. Step 2: E is the mother of D, so D is a child of A and E. Step 3: B is the brother of C and C is the sister of D. So B, C, and D are siblings. Step 4: There is only one daughter of the husband of E (i.e., A). So A has only one daughter. Since C is the sister of D and B is the brother of C, D must be the only daughter. Therefore, D is female and B is male. Step 5: D and B are siblings, so B is the brother of D.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Sister) would mean B is female. Option C is incorrect. Option D (Cousin) is wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, Rekha says to Lalli, 'The girl in the photo is the second daughter of the wife of only son of the grandmother of my younger sister.' How is this girl of photograph related to Rekha?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'Grandmother of my younger sister' is Rekha's grandmother. Step 2: 'Only son of grandmother' is Rekha's father. Step 3: 'Wife of only son' is Rekha's mother. Step 4: 'Second daughter of the wife' is Rekha's sister. Therefore, the girl is Rekha's sister.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Cousin) would be uncle/aunt's child. Option C (Niece) would be sibling's daughter. Option D (Daughter) is wrong generation.

mcqs.q: Ravi was at a family gathering when he pointed to a woman sitting across the room and said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' Everyone turned to look at the woman. How is the woman related to Ravi?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Ravi's grandfather's only son is Ravi's father. Step 2: The woman is the daughter of Ravi's father. Step 3: Therefore, the woman is Ravi's sister.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would mean she is his parent. Option C (Aunt) would mean she is his parent's sister. Option D (Cousin) would be uncle/aunt's child.
mcqs.trap: Assuming the woman is Ravi's mother.

mcqs.q: While flipping through an old photo album, Raj paused at a photograph of a man and said, 'I have no brothers and sisters, but the man's father is my father's son.' His friend looked puzzled. Who is the man in the photograph?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Raj has no siblings, so 'my father's son' refers to Raj himself. Step 2: The man's father is Raj, so the man in the photograph is Raj's son.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Raj's Father) would mean Raj's father is Raj's father's son (impossible). Option C (Raj himself) would mean Raj's father is Raj. Option D (Grandfather) is wrong.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'my father's son' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: At a wedding, Ramesh pointed to a woman dressed in a bright red saree and said to his friend, 'She is the wife of my mother's only son.' His friend nodded in understanding. How is the woman related to Ramesh?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Ramesh's mother's only son is Ramesh himself. Step 2: The woman is the wife of Ramesh. Therefore, the woman is Ramesh's wife.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Sister) would mean she is his sibling. Option B (Mother) would mean she is his parent. Option D (Sister-in-law) would be brother's wife or wife's sister.
mcqs.trap: Assuming 'only son' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: If A is the brother of B, B is the sister of C, and C is the father of D, how is A related to D?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: A is the brother of B, and B is the sister of C, so A is the brother of C. Step 2: C is the father of D. Step 3: A is the brother of D's father, so A is D's uncle.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Father) would mean A is D's parent. Option C (Brother) is same generation. Option D (Grandfather) is two generations above.
mcqs.trap: Forgetting that A is C's brother, not C.

mcqs.q: If Esha is Mother of Karan, Karan is Grandson of Seema, Seema is Mother of Bharat, Bharat is Son of Sanjay, Sanjay is Father of Abhishek. Esha is _______________ of Abhishek.
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Let's map in reference to Abhishek. Sanjay is Father of Abhishek. Step 2: Bharat is Son of Sanjay, so Bharat is brother of Abhishek. Step 3: Seema is Mother of Bharat, so Seema is Mother of Abhishek as well. Step 4: Karan is Grandson of Seema, so Karan is son of Abhishek or Bharat. Step 5: Esha is Mother of Karan, so Esha is wife of Abhishek (if Karan is Abhishek's son) or wife of Bharat. Since the chain suggests Karan is Abhishek's son (as Abhishek is son of Sanjay and brother of Bharat), Esha is wife of Abhishek.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would mean Esha is Abhishek's mother. Option B (Sister) is same generation. Option D (Daughter-in-law) would mean Esha is married to Abhishek's son.
mcqs.trap: Getting lost in the chain of relationships.

mcqs.q: While watching kids play in the park, a man pointed to a boy and said to his friend, 'He is the son of my grandfather's only daughter.' How is the boy related to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The man's grandfather's only daughter is the man's mother. Step 2: The boy is the son of the man's mother. Step 3: Therefore, the boy is the man's brother.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Son) would mean the boy is the man's child. Option C (Nephew) would be sibling's son. Option D (Cousin) would be uncle/aunt's child.
mcqs.trap: Assuming 'only daughter' is the man's aunt instead of mother.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a woman at a family gathering, Raju said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son-in-law.' How is the woman related to Raju?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Raju's grandfather's only son-in-law is Raju's father. Step 2: The woman is the daughter of Raju's father. Step 3: Therefore, the woman is Raju's sister.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Cousin) would be uncle/aunt's child. Option C (Aunt) would be parent's sister. Option D (Mother) would be parent.

mcqs.q: R and S are brothers, X is the sister of Y and X is the mother of R. What is Y to S?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: X is the mother of R. Step 2: R and S are brothers, so X is also the mother of S. Step 3: X is the sister of Y. Step 4: Therefore, Y is the sibling of S's mother. Step 5: So Y is S's maternal uncle (if male) or maternal aunt (if female). Since the options don't have 'Aunt' and the question asks 'Y to S' with given options, and Y's gender is not specified but the options include 'Uncle', 'Mother', 'Father', 'Brother', the correct answer is Uncle (assuming Y is male).
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Mother) would mean Y is S's mother. Option C (Father) would mean Y is S's father. Option D (Brother) would mean Y is S's sibling.

mcqs.q: A woman pointing to a man said, 'He is the widower of my uncle's brother's daughter.' How is the man related to the woman?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My uncle's brother' could be the woman's father or another uncle. Step 2: 'Brother's daughter' is the niece of the brother. Step 3: The man is the widower of the woman's cousin. Step 4: The widower of one's cousin is one's cousin's husband. That would make him the woman's brother-in-law (if the cousin is the woman's sister) or cousin-in-law. But given the options, the most appropriate is Brother-in-law.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Brother) is same generation sibling. Option C (Nephew) is sibling's son. Option D (Husband) is spouse.

mcqs.q: Pointing to Vinod, Radha said, 'His sister is my mother's only daughter'. How is Radha related to Vinod?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'My mother's only daughter' is Radha herself. Step 2: Vinod's sister is Radha. Step 3: Therefore, Radha is Vinod's sister.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Cousin) would be uncle/aunt's child. Option B (Daughter) would mean Vinod is her parent. Option D (Mother) would mean Vinod is her child.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'only daughter' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: Introducing a man to her husband, a woman said, 'His brother's father is the only son of my grandfather.' How is the woman related to this man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The only son of the woman's grandfather is the woman's father. Step 2: The man's brother's father is the man's father. Step 3: Man's father = Woman's father. Step 4: Therefore, the man and woman are siblings. So the woman is the man's sister.
mcqs.elimination: Option B (Mother) would mean she is his parent. Option C (Aunt) would mean she is his parent's sister. Option D (Daughter) would reverse the relationship.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'only son' refers to the man himself.

mcqs.exp: Step 1: The information provided is incomplete. 'Q and T is the father of a trader' is unclear - does it mean Q is the father and T is also the father? Or does it mean Q and T are the parents? Step 2: Without complete information about the relationships, we cannot determine the relation.
mcqs.elimination: All other options require more information.

mcqs.exp: Step 1: Akshay is Sonia's son. Step 2: Priyank is Akshay's brother, so Priyank is also Sonia's son. Step 3: Sonia is Saksham's sister. Step 4: Therefore, Saksham is the maternal uncle of Priyank (since Saksham is Sonia's brother, and Sonia is Priyank's mother). Step 5: So Priyank is Saksham's nephew.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Brother) would mean same generation. Option B (Son) would mean Saksham is his parent. Option D is incorrect as data is sufficient.

mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'Only brother of my wife' is Vijay's brother-in-law. Step 2: 'Son of only brother of my wife' is Vijay's nephew (wife's brother's son). Step 3: Rukmani is the mother of that son, so Rukmani is the wife of Vijay's brother-in-law. Step 4: Rukmani's husband is Vijay's brother-in-law. Therefore, Rukmani's husband is Vijay's brother-in-law.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Brother) would mean he is Vijay's sibling. Option C (Father) is wrong generation. Option D (Uncle) is parent's sibling.

mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would mean P is R's child. Option C (Aunt) is wrong. Option D (Sister) is same generation.

mcqs.q: A man said to a woman, 'The only son of your brother is the brother of my wife.' How is that woman related to the wife of that man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The woman's brother's son is the woman's nephew. Step 2: That nephew is the brother of the man's wife. Step 3: So the man's wife is the sister of the woman's nephew. Step 4: Therefore, the man's wife is the daughter of the woman's brother. Step 5: So the woman is the aunt of the man's wife.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Mother) would mean the woman is the man's wife's mother. Option B (Sister) is same generation. Option D (Mother-in-law) is spouse's mother.
mcqs.trap: Confusing the direction of the relationship.

mcqs.q: If D is the brother of B, how B is related to C? To answer this question which of the statements is/are necessary? I. The son of D is the grandson of C. II. B is the sister of D.
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Statement I: The son of D is the grandson of C. This means C is the parent of D (since D's son is C's grandson). Step 2: Statement II: B is the sister of D. This tells us B is female and sibling of D. Step 3: Using both statements, we know C is parent of D, and D is brother of B. So B is also child of C. Therefore, B is the daughter of C. Step 4: Statement I alone doesn't give B's gender (B could be son or daughter of C). Statement II alone doesn't give C's relation to B (C could be parent, grandparent, etc.). So both are necessary.
mcqs.elimination: Option A (Only I) doesn't give B's gender. Option B (Only II) doesn't give C's relation to B. Option D is incorrect.

mcqs.q: A + B means A is the mother of B. A - B means A is the brother of B. A × B means A is the daughter of B. Which of the following shows that E is the maternal uncle of D?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: We need E to be the maternal uncle of D. So E must be male (brother of D's mother). Step 2: Option A: E - F means E is the brother of F. F + D means F is the mother of D. So E is the brother of D's mother. That makes E the maternal uncle of D. Step 3: Option B: E - F means E is brother of F. F × D means F is daughter of D. This doesn't work. Option C: E + F means E is mother of F (E is female). Option D: E × F means E is daughter of F (E is female).
mcqs.elimination: Option B, C, and D don't give the correct relationship.
mcqs.trap: Confusing maternal uncle with paternal uncle.

Step 4: Arjun could be the child of either Raghav or Devansh. Step 5: Without more specific information, we cannot determine who Arjun's parent is.

mcqs.exp: Step 1: A is B's mother. Step 2: C is B's father. Step 3: A and C are spouses. Step 4: D is C's sister. Step 5: D is A's sister-in-law (husband's sister).
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If X is Y's father, Z is Y's mother, W is Z's brother, how is W related to X?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: X is Y's father. Step 2: Z is Y's mother. Step 3: X and Z are spouses. Step 4: W is Z's brother. Step 5: W is X's brother-in-law (wife's brother).
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If P is Q's sister, R is Q's brother, S is R's wife, how is S related to P?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: P, Q, and R are siblings. Step 2: S is R's wife. Step 3: S is P's sister-in-law (brother's wife).
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If A is B's brother, C is B's sister, D is C's husband, how is D related to A?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: A, B, and C are siblings. Step 2: D is C's husband. Step 3: D is A's brother-in-law (sister's husband).
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If M is N's father, O is N's mother, P is O's father, how is P related to M?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: M is N's father. Step 2: O is N's mother. Step 3: M and O are spouses. Step 4: P is O's father. Step 5: P is M's father-in-law (wife's father).
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If X is Y's mother, Z is Y's father, W is Z's mother, how is W related to X?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: X is Y's mother. Step 2: Z is Y's father. Step 3: X and Z are spouses. Step 4: W is Z's mother. Step 5: W is X's mother-in-law (husband's mother).
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my mother's only son.' How is the person in the photograph related to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'my mother's only son' = the man himself. Step 2: 'She is the daughter of me'. Step 3: Therefore, she is his daughter.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a woman said, 'He is the son of my father's only daughter.' How is the person in the photograph related to the woman?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'my father's only daughter' = the woman herself. Step 2: 'He is the son of me'. Step 3: Therefore, he is her son.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the wife of my father's only son.' How is the person in the photograph related to the man?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'my father's only son' = the man himself. Step 2: 'She is the wife of me'. Step 3: Therefore, she is his wife.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a woman said, 'He is the husband of my mother's only daughter.' How is the person in the photograph related to the woman?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: 'my mother's only daughter' = the woman herself. Step 2: 'He is the husband of me'. Step 3: Therefore, he is her husband.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If A + B means A is the sister of B, A - B means A is the father of B, A × B means A is the mother of B. What is P + Q - R × S?
opts: P is the grandmother of S
P is the aunt of S
P is the mother of S
mcqs.exp: Step 1: P + Q = P is sister of Q. Step 2: Q - R = Q is father of R. Step 3: R × S = R is mother of S. Step 4: Q is father of R, and R is mother of S, so Q is grandfather of S. Step 5: P is sister of Q, so P is the aunt of S (since P is the sister of S's grandfather).
mcqs.elimination: Options A, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If A @ B means A is the mother of B, A # B means A is the father of B, A $ B means A is the brother of B. What is P @ Q $ R # S?
opts: P is the grandmother of S
P is the mother of S
P is the aunt of S
mcqs.exp: Step 1: P @ Q = P is mother of Q. Step 2: Q $ R = Q is brother of R. Step 3: R # S = R is father of S. Step 4: Q is brother of R, so Q is also child of P. R is child of P as well. R is father of S, so S is grandchild of P. Step 5: P is the grandmother of S.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If A % B means A is the daughter of B, A & B means A is the son of B, A $ B means A is the sister of B. What is P % Q $ R & S?
opts: P is the granddaughter of S
P is the daughter of S
P is the niece of S
mcqs.exp: Step 1: P % Q = P is daughter of Q. Step 2: Q $ R = Q is sister of R. Step 3: R & S = R is son of S. Step 4: Q is sister of R, so Q and R are siblings. R is son of S, so S is parent of R and Q. Step 5: P is daughter of Q, so P is grandchild of S. Since P is female (daughter), P is the granddaughter of S.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'I have no brother or sister, but that man's father is my father's son.' Whose photograph was it?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The man has no siblings, so 'my father's son' is the man himself. Step 2: 'That man's father is me'. Step 3: Therefore, the man in the photo is his son.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a woman said, 'I have no sisters, but that woman's mother is my mother's only daughter.' Whose photograph was it?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: The woman has no sisters, so 'my mother's only daughter' is the woman herself. Step 2: 'That woman's mother is me'. Step 3: Therefore, the woman in the photo is her daughter.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: A is B's father. B is C's sister. C is D's brother. How is A related to D?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: B and C are siblings (B is sister of C, C is brother of D, so B, C, and D are siblings). Step 2: A is B's father. Step 3: A is also father of C and D. Therefore, A is the father of D.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: X is Y's mother. Y is Z's brother. Z is W's sister. How is X related to W?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: Y, Z, and W are siblings (Y is Z's brother, Z is W's sister). Step 2: X is Y's mother. Step 3: X is also mother of Z and W. Therefore, X is the mother of W.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: P is Q's son. Q is R's daughter. R is S's father. How is P related to S?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: P is Q's son. Step 2: Q is R's daughter. Step 3: R is S's father. Step 4: S is R's father (since R is S's father, S is the father of R). Step 5: Q is R's daughter, so Q is S's granddaughter. Step 6: P is Q's son, so P is S's great-grandson.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: A is B's grandfather. B is C's father. C is D's brother. How is A related to D?
mcqs.exp: Step 1: A is B's grandfather. Step 2: B is C's father. Step 3: C is D's brother, so C and D are siblings. Step 4: B is father of C and D. Step 5: A is grandfather of B. Step 6: A is great-grandfather of C and D. Therefore, A is the great-grandfather of D.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Introducing a woman, a man said, 'Her husband is the only son of my father'. How is that woman related to the man?
mcqs.exp: The man's father has only one son, who is the man himself. That son is the husband of the woman. Therefore, the woman is the man's wife.[reference:80]
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.
mcqs.trap: Assuming 'only son' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His father is the only son of your mother'. How is the man related to the woman?
mcqs.exp: The woman's mother has only one son, who is the woman herself? Actually, the statement is 'His father is the only son of your mother.' So the man's father is the only son of the woman's mother. That means the man's father is the woman's brother. So the man is the woman's nephew. But 'Nephew' is not an option. Let me re-read: The question says 'Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His father is the only son of your mother'.' Here 'your' refers to the woman being addressed. So the man's father is the only son of the woman being addressed's mother. That makes the man's father the brother of the woman being addressed. So the man is the nephew of the woman being addressed. But the options are Son, Brother, Husband, Father. None of these is 'Nephew'. This might be a trick question. The correct answer is 'Son' if we interpret it differently: If the woman being addressed is the mother of the man, then his father is her husband, and her husband is the only son of her mother? That doesn't work. Let me re-evaluate: 'Pointing to a man, a woman said to a man, 'His father is the only son of your mother'.' So the second man's mother has only one son, who is the second man himself. So the first man's father is the second man. So the first man is the son of the second man. Therefore, the man being pointed to is the son of the man being addressed.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If A + B means A is the mother of B, A - B means A is the brother of B, A × B means A is the daughter of B. Which of the following means that P is the grandmother of Q?
mcqs.exp: P + R means P is mother of R. R × Q means R is daughter of Q. So R is daughter of Q, and P is mother of R. So P is grandmother of Q.[reference:81]
mcqs.elimination: Options A, B, and C give different relationships.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a lady, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my father's only son.' How is the lady related to the man?
mcqs.exp: 'My father's only son' is the man himself. So the lady is the daughter of the man. Therefore, she is his daughter.[reference:82]
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.
mcqs.trap: Thinking 'only son' refers to someone else.

mcqs.q: A is the father of B. C is the daughter of B. D is the brother of C. How is A related to D?
mcqs.exp: A is father of B. C is daughter of B, so C is granddaughter of A. D is brother of C, so D is also grandchild of A. Therefore, A is the grandfather of D.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: P is the son of Q. Q is the daughter of R. R is the father of S. How is P related to S?
mcqs.exp: P is son of Q. Q is daughter of R, so Q is child of R. R is father of S, so S is sibling of Q. P is child of Q, so P is grandchild of R and S is child of R. So P is the nephew of S? Actually, S is the sibling of Q, so S is the uncle/aunt of P. So P is the nephew/niece of S. But the options are Grandson, Son, Nephew, Great-grandson. The correct answer is Nephew.
mcqs.elimination: Options A, B, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: If A is B's brother, C is B's mother, D is C's father, how is D related to A?
mcqs.exp: A and B are siblings. C is the mother of A and B. D is C's father. So D is the grandfather of A.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Introducing a woman, a man said, 'She is the mother of my wife's daughter.' How is the woman related to the man?
mcqs.exp: 'My wife's daughter' could be the man's daughter or step-daughter. The mother of the daughter is the man's wife. So the woman is the man's wife.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: Pointing to a photograph, a woman said, 'He is the only son of my father's daughter.' How is the person in the photograph related to the woman?
mcqs.exp: 'My father's daughter' could be the woman herself or her sister. If it's the woman herself, then the person is her son. But 'Son' is not an option. If it's her sister, then the person is her nephew. Since 'Nephew' is an option, the answer is Nephew.
mcqs.elimination: Options A, B, and D are wrong.

mcqs.q: A is B's father. B is C's brother. C is D's sister. How is A related to D?
mcqs.exp: B, C, and D are siblings (B is C's brother, C is D's sister). A is B's father. So A is also father of C and D. Therefore, A is the father of D.
mcqs.elimination: Options B, C, and D are wrong.
